architecture_summary: DehazeFormer is a modified 5-stage U-Net based on Swin Transformer, with RescaleNorm, ReLU/SoftReLU-style activation choices, shifted window partitioning with reflection padding, parallel convolution with attention, SK fusion, and a soft reconstruction module for single image dehazing.

novel_contributions: We propose DehazeFormer, an image dehazing Transformer inspired by Swin Transformer with several critical modifications for image dehazing.
We propose RescaleNorm to replace LayerNorm and argue that LayerNorm harms image dehazing performance.
We propose SoftReLU and find ReLU / LeakyReLU work better than GELU for image dehazing.
We propose a shifted window partitioning scheme based on reflection padding and cropping to keep constant window size at image edges.
We propose a spatial information aggregation scheme that combines MHSA with convolution in parallel.
We propose a prior-based soft reconstruction module and an SK fusion module.
We collect a large-scale realistic remote sensing dehazing dataset named RS-Haze.

key_claims.claim: DehazeFormer is a modified Swin Transformer-based U-Net for single image dehazing, with improvements in normalization, activation, window partitioning, aggregation, fusion, and reconstruction.
On the SOTS indoor set, the small model outperforms FFA-Net with only 25% #Param and 5% computational cost.
The large model is the first method with the PSNR over 40 dB on the SOTS indoor set.
DehazeFormer-B surpasses all baseline methods in terms of PSNR and SSIM on the RESIDE-Full indoor set.
DehazeFormer-S surpasses FFA-Net on RS-Haze, and the dataset is designed to evaluate highly non-homogeneous haze removal.
key_claims.evidence_section: IV. RS-HAZE DATASET; V. B. Quantitative Comparison

key_claims.claim: RescaleNorm, reflection-padding-based shifted window partitioning, and parallel convolution each improve performance in ablation studies.
